title: UAE's departure from the OPEC oil cartel is not without precedence. Who could be next?
summary: The United Arab Emirates' decision to leave OPEC is reverberating across global energy markets, sparking questions on who else could follow.

title: Home REIT shares restored to London Stock Exchange listing

summary: China has allowed its state energy companies to export more fuels next month after a temporary suspension of all such exports in March. title: Crude Inventories Continue to Decline Amid Strong Oil Product Draws
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 1.79 million barrels in the week ending April 24. title: Higher Oil Prices Lift Sinopec Profit 28% in First Quarter
summary: China’s top refiner posted a strong first quarter as higher crude prices lifted inventory values and stable domestic fuel sales helped to offset pressure from disrupted Middle East supply. title: China’s Data Center Boom Could Double Power Demand by 2030
summary: China is on course to nearly double its data center capacity within five years, with 28 gigawatts (GW) of new projects expected to come online by 2030, adding to the 32 GW already installed as of the end of last year, according to new analysis from Rystad Energy. title: UAE to leave Opec in blow to oil cartel
summary: Move underlines long-running frustrations with group over production quotas
